message.content: Yesterday you ran across a bug where there appears to be a lock not being released whan trying to do `vc-x1 push`, I have a guess we are spawning a `git` command instead of a `jj` command. Thoughts

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Read the lock-race bug entry

content.content:
a locally-init'd project's `remote-code.git` looks for
     `remote-code.claude.git` and fails.
   - A relative local-path TARGET fails on the session side
     regardless: `clone_dual` runs the session `jj git
     clone` with the just-cloned code repo as cwd, so the
     relative source no longer resolves. Workaround: pass an
     absolute TARGET.

3. **`push` `bookmark-set` races the git index lock.**
   `jj bookmark set` on the colocated work repo failed twice
   (the 0.69.0-3 and 0.69.0-4 pushes, same stage) with
   "Failed to reset Git HEAD state … could not acquire lock
   for `.git/index` … after 1 attempt(s)"; the lockfile was
   already gone on inspection seconds later.
   - **Cost:** push aborts mid-flow (rollback restored both
     repos cleanly both times); recovery is a `--restart`
     rerun, which succeeded both times.
   - The bot thinks a git-aware watcher (shell prompt,
     editor) briefly re-reads the repo after the commit
     stages touch `.git`, holding the index lock exactly
     when `bookmark-set` resets git HEAD; jj gives up after
     a single attempt.
   - **Fix direction** (two options, possibly combined):
     - retry with short backoff around the bookmark-set
       stage (or all jj invocations that reset git HEAD
       state)
     - use jj-lib in-process instead of spawning `jj`
       commands (suggested 2026-07-15) — the lock
       contention is external, so this alone doesn't
       remove the race, but the retry loop becomes ours
       (catch the lock error, back off, retry) with real
       error types instead of stderr parsing; overlaps the
       jj-facade and jj-lib-vs-subprocess Todos

# References

content.text:
Your guess doesn't hold up — the push path spawns only `jj`, never raw `git`. Here's what I found.

**The bug is already recorded** as entry 3 in `notes/bugs.md` ("`push` `bookmark-set` races the git index lock", from the 0.69.0-3 and 0.69.0-4 pushes).

**The failing stage spawns jj.** `stage_bookmark_set` (src/push.rs:1222) runs `jj bookmark set <bk> -r @- -R <repo>` through the common `run()` helper, and I checked every `run()` call site in `push.rs` — all ten spawn `"jj"`. The raw-`git` invocations in the codebase live in `init.rs`, `repo_utils.rs`, and `clone.rs` (setup/verification paths), not in push.

**Why the error still mentions git:** both repos are colocated, so jj itself maintains the git side — `jj bookmark set` moves the bookmark and then resets git's HEAD/index to match, which requires taking `.git/index.lock` internally (via jj's embedded git library, not a spawned `git` process). The error string "Failed to reset Git HEAD state … could not acquire lock for `.git/index` … after 1 attempt(s)" is jj's own message, and "after 1 attempt(s)" is the tell: jj tries once and gives up. So a git-looking error from a jj-only pipeline is expected in colocated mode.

**Who holds the lock, then?** It's not us — `run()` uses `.output()`, which blocks until each jj command fully exits, so the prior commit stages can't still be holding it. The bugs.md hypothesis stands: something external and git-aware (shell prompt integration like starship, an editor/IDE, or a git fsmonitor) briefly grabs `.git/index` right after the commit stages touch the repo, exactly when bookmark-set wants it. That also explains why the lockfile was gone seconds later and why `--restart` succeeded both times.

**My recommendation** matches the first fix direction already noted in bugs.md: a retry-with-short-backoff wrapper around the jj invocations that reset git HEAD state (detect the "could not acquire lock" stderr, sleep ~100–500ms, retry a few times). It's small, targets the actual failure, and there's even precedent in the codebase — `src/options_flags/README.md` sketches a `run_retry(...)` shape. The jj-lib in-process option is a bigger lift and doesn't remove the race by itself (the contention is external); it just makes the retry loop cleaner, so I'd treat it as the existing jj-facade Todo's concern, not this bug's fix.

Want me to pick this up as a cycle (retry wrapper around the lock-sensitive jj calls), or just update the bugs.md entry to rule out the spawned-git theory?

aiTitle: Debug vc-x1 push lock release issue

content.text:
Short answer: mostly yes — nearly all the raw-`git` spawns sit in a window where jj deliberately doesn't exist, or do something jj has no equivalent for. And none of them run during `push`, so they're unrelated to the lock bug. Breakdown:

**Necessary — no jj repo exists at that moment.** `init`'s `push_repo` (src/init.rs:1389-1429) deliberately strips jj ("Step 7: Setting bookmark and removing jj"), does the remote work git-only, then re-colocates jj in Step 10. Inside that window:
- `git checkout main` (init.rs:1403) — re-attaches HEAD after the clean; jj is gone.
- `git remote add origin` (init.rs:1482) — `jj git remote add` exists, but not without a jj repo.
- `git push -u origin main` (init.rs:1484) — same window; also sets upstream tracking that the later `jj bookmark track` relies on.

**Necessary — jj has no equivalent.**
- `git clean -xdf` (init.rs:1395/1399) — its whole job is deleting `.jj/` state plus untracked files; jj can't strip itself.
- `git init --bare` (init.rs:1471) — jj has no bare-repo concept; this creates the local origin.

**Replaceable but harmless.**
- `git rev-parse HEAD` (repo_utils.rs:162,164 and init.rs:1236) — debug-only logging. In a colocated repo `jj log -r @- -T commit_id --no-graph` gives the same SHA, though the point of these lines is precisely to surface the *git-side* hash, so raw git is arguably the honest tool here.

**Possibly redundant.**
- `git init` before `jj git init --colocate` (repo_utils.rs:77-79) — modern jj creates the git repo itself when you `git init --colocate` in an empty directory, so the explicit `git init` is likely a no-op. It does pin the git default-branch behavior independent of jj's version, which may be why it's there.

**Tests** (repo_utils.rs:188,211) spawn `git log` inside `#[cfg(test)]` — intentional, since they're verifying the git side of the colocated repo.

The rest of the grep hits were false positives: `clone.rs:191`, `sync.rs:438`, and `squash_push.rs:422` all spawn `jj` with `git` as a subcommand (`jj git clone/fetch/push`).

So the codebase's git usage is confined to `init`/setup where it's structural, and the push path — where your lock bug lives — is pure jj. The bug remains what bugs.md entry 3 says: jj's own single-attempt index-lock acquisition losing a race to an external process.

aiTitle: Debug vc-x1 push lock release issue

content.text:
I couldn't read the share link (the page is a JS app that doesn't render for fetching), but I dug into the project's own history instead — and your sibling is right, with one small exception. The strip-jj dance turns out to be a vestige.

**Why the raw git is there: the submodule era.** `notes/chores/chores-03.md:171-180` records the original init design: jj had to be *removed* (`git clean -xdf` deletes `.jj/`) because step 7 was `git submodule add` — a git-only operation jj doesn't support — and jj was re-colocated only at the end. The submodule linkage was later replaced by the `.claude` symlink (today's Step 11), but the strip-jj → git-remote-add → git-push → re-colocate scaffolding survived the redesign. With no submodule step in the middle, the reason for evicting jj is gone.

**The project already proves the jj-only path works.** `notes/chores/chores-06.md:170-174` documents that `test-fixture`'s Step 7 pushes with `jj git push --bookmark main` directly and confirms tracking gets established as a side effect — verified via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a fresh fixture. So a jj-only init would be roughly: `jj git init --colocate` (which creates the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` at repo_utils.rs:77 is already redundant), commit, `gh repo create`, `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj git push --bookmark main`. That eliminates the `git clean`, `git checkout`, `git remote add`, and `git push -u` — and the entire Step 7/Step 10 strip-and-re-init pair collapses. The `git rev-parse HEAD` debug lines are replaceable with a `jj log -T commit_id` template.

**The one real exception:** `git init --bare` (init.rs:1471) for the `LocalBareInit` provisioner. jj cannot create a bare git repo — there's no jj concept for it. The GitHub path avoids this via `gh`, but the local-origin path keeps one genuine raw-git call unless that provisioner changes shape.

So my earlier "necessary given the sequence" was accurate but circular — the sequence itself is the vestige. A "de-gitify init" change would simplify `push_repo` substantially, and as a bonus removes the `git checkout`/`git clean` operations that touch `.git/index` during init (though the push-time lock bug is a separate, external race).

Want me to add a Todo entry for this in `notes/todo.md` (named by title, with a ref to the chores-03 submodule-era context)?

message.content:
Here is my original Q:

Hoe to create a jj colocated git repo locally, initialize it with some files,
create the initial commit and then push it to GitHub using gh or other cli
to push to a non existent remote repository which git can't do anyway.

Here is claude.ai answer:

```bash
# 1. Create the colocated repo
mkdir myproject && cd myproject
jj git init --colocate

# 2. Add some files
echo "# myproject" > README.md
cargo init .   # or whatever

# 3. Snapshot and describe the work
jj describe -m "Initial commit"

# 4. Create the GitHub repo and add it as remote
gh repo create myproject --private --source=. --remote=origin

# 5. Point a bookmark at your commit and push
jj bookmark create main -r @
jj git push --bookmark main --allow-new
```

A few notes:

- `gh repo create --source=. --remote=origin` creates the repo on GitHub *and* wires up the remote in one step — this is the part plain git can't do. Add `--push` and it will push via git directly, but since you're in jj-land it's cleaner to let jj do the push so its bookmark tracking stays consistent.
- `--allow-new` is required the first time you push a bookmark that doesn't exist on the remote (newer jj versions; older ones used `--allow-new` only after it became opt-in).
- If you'd rather your described commit be "finished" before pushing, do `jj new` after step 3 so `@` is a fresh empty change and point the bookmark at `@-` instead: `jj bookmark create main -r @-`.
- After the first push, `jj git push` alone will push tracked bookmarks; subsequent moves of `main` (`jj bookmark move main --to @-`) push without `--allow-new`.

Since it's colocated, `git status`, `gh`, and any git tooling all see the same repo, and jj auto-imports/exports refs on each command.

content.text: That answer matches what the repo's own history shows — and it gives me something concrete to verify. Before writing anything down, let me empirically test the jj-only flow with the locally installed jj, including whether `--allow-new` is actually needed (AGENTS.md claims jj pushes new bookmarks without special flags, which contradicts web-claude's note — worth settling).

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).

2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the

content.content:
298	15. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
299	    `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
300	    its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
301	    so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
302	    on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field

15. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
    `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
    its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
    so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
    on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
    leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
    `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
    (`init.rs`, tests).

    Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
    of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
    issue.
16. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
    companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
    workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
    setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
    proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
    Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
17. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization.**
    Replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
    subcommands (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`)
    with a scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot`
    when absent. Body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit/design
    work [[13]] identified this as the cheapest concrete
    equalization and the right prototype for the
    topology-from-config rule (subcommand reads topology
    from `default_scope`, not from a flag). Validates the
    broader design before larger pieces (`push`,
    `--init-from*`) commit to it. The remaining 13
    implementation gaps live in [[13]]'s `## Gap list` for
    future Preparation passes to pick up.

18. **De-gitify `init`: strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate is
    a submodule-era vestige.** `init.rs::push_repo` evicts jj
    (`git clean -xdf` + `git checkout main`), runs
    `git remote add` + `git push -u` raw, then re-colocates —
    the step shape the abandoned submodule design required
    [[2]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.
    - jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
      the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
      `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
      `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
      -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`. No `--allow-new`
      needed; push established tracking as a side effect
      (matches the test-fixture observation in [[3]]).
    - Collapses Step 7/Step 10 into the main flow; drops
      `git clean` / `git checkout` / `git remote add` /
      `git push -u`; the `git rev-parse HEAD` debug lines can
      become `jj log -T commit_id` queries.
    - Exception: `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` stays —
      jj cannot create a bare repo. The GitHub path needs no
      raw git: `gh repo create` provisions, jj pushes.

## Ideas

content.text:
The jj-only flow is verified and captured. Summary of where this landed:

**Empirical check (jj 0.40.0, scratch run):** I built a colocated repo and pushed to a local bare stand-in for GitHub using only jj — `jj git init --colocate <dest>`, write files, `jj commit`, `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`. It worked end-to-end: the commit landed on the remote and `jj bookmark list --tracked` confirms tracking was established by the push itself.

**Two deltas from web-claude's answer, both version/style:**
- `--allow-new` is **not** needed on jj 0.40 — the new-bookmark push succeeded without it, so AGENTS.md's "no `--allow-new`" note is correct for this environment; that caveat belongs to older jj versions.
- Its `jj describe` + `jj bookmark create main -r @` variant maps to this project's convention as `jj commit` + `-r @-` (which is exactly its own "if you'd rather the commit be finished" footnote).

**Also confirmed the historical why:** `chores-03.md` records that the strip-jj dance existed because the original design used `git submodule add` — a git-only step. The symlink design replaced submodules, but the scaffolding stayed.

**Recorded as Todo #18** in `notes/todo.md` — "De-gitify `init`: strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate is a submodule-era vestige" — with refs to the chores-03 submodule-era section and the chores-06 tracking observation, the verified command sequence, and the one true exception: `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare`, since jj can't create bare repos (the GitHub path needs no raw git at all — `gh repo create` provisions, jj pushes). `vc-x1 validate-todo` passes with the new entry.

9. **Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a typed facade.**
   ~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
   "--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
   module has quietly grown a private wrapper:
   `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
   rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` plus four
   inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
   three template variants in `sync.rs`. Spotted at 0.69.0-2:
   `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is the
   Nth reinvention.
   - one facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a
     `common` split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus
     typed helpers — `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`,
     `desc_of`, `is_empty`
   - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command`
     + its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
     `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking
     parsers will eventually disagree
   - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
     `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
     (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
     `desc_helpers` both can call
   - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()`
     / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
     `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
     and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
10. **Split push.rs state machinery; document the jj-lib vs
    subprocess rule.** `push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest
    file) holds the `Stage` machine, TOML state persistence,
    eight stage bodies, two sanity verifiers, and the
    interactive gates; `sync/state.rs` is the in-repo
    precedent for splitting.
    - extract `push/state.rs`: `Stage`, `PushState`, state
      layout resolution
    - ARCHITECTURE.md: state the implicit access rule —
      read-only display commands (`chid`, `desc`, `list`,
      `show`, `validate-desc`) use jj-lib in-process;
      mutating commands (`init`, `sync`, `push`,
      `squash-push`) shell out to `jj` — so new subcommands
      don't re-decide it ad hoc
11. **Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
    hardcoded `.claude`).** The bot repo lives at `.claude`

84	3. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
85	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
86	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
87	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
88	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
89	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
90	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
91	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
92	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
93	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
94	4. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
95	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
96	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
97	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
98	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
99	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
100	   single-mode sync cycle.
101	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
102	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
103	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
104	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
105	     nothing invokes it.
106	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
107	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
108	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).

file.content:
3. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
4. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
   single-mode sync cycle.
   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
     halts the push before anything is committed); the
     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
     nothing invokes it.
   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).

input.old_string:
18. **De-gitify `init`: strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate is
    a submodule-era vestige.** `init.rs::push_repo` evicts jj
    (`git clean -xdf` + `git checkout main`), runs
    `git remote add` + `git push -u` raw, then re-colocates —
    the step shape the abandoned submodule design required
    [[2]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.
    - jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
      the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
      `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
      `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
      -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`. No `--allow-new`
      needed; push established tracking as a side effect
      (matches the test-fixture observation in [[3]]).
    - Collapses Step 7/Step 10 into the main flow; drops
      `git clean` / `git checkout` / `git remote add` /
      `git push -u`; the `git rev-parse HEAD` debug lines can
      become `jj log -T commit_id` queries.
    - Exception: `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` stays —
      jj cannot create a bare repo. The GitHub path needs no
      raw git: `gh repo create` provisions, jj pushes.
input.new_string:
18. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
    subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
    scattered hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query
    sites plus every mutation) with per-module private
    wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in init. Spawning means
    stderr parsing instead of typed errors, and jj's
    single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our
    control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race in
    [bugs.md](bugs.md) [[4]] can't be retried where it
    fails. Absorbs the former "Dedup jj subprocess queries
    behind a typed facade" Todo. Rough staging:
    - **DRY behind one facade module** (name open:
      `src/jj.rs` or a `common` split): `jj_log(repo, rev,
      template)` plus typed helpers — `rev_exists`,
      `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`. Existing
      reinventions to fold: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists,
      jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
      `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline
      `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
      template variants in `sync.rs`.
      - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
        `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
        parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
        two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
      - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
        `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
        `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
        one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
      - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
        `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
        `push/integration_tests.rs`,
        `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
        `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
    - **De-gitify init.** `init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj →
      git-push → re-colocate flow is the step shape the
      abandoned submodule design required [[2]]; the
      symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
      replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
      the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
      `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
      `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set
      main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`; no
      `--allow-new` needed, push established tracking as a
      side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
      [[3]]). Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
      `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
    - **Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib** (already a
      dependency for read-only commands): reads first
      (`jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors), then
      mutations (commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
      fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store
      lift). Removes jj and git spawning entirely; the
      index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
      retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for
      bugs.md entry 3 [[4]]. `LocalBareInit`'s
      `git init --bare` becomes a gix call (jj-lib already
      pulls gix in).
    - **Update the access-rule doc** (moved from the "Split
      push.rs state machinery" Todo): today's implicit rule
      — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
      commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
      in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
      instead.
    - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
      `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
      until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
      lands (same direction, independently shippable).
      Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
      invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
      in-process read.
    - Design risk (the bot thinks): in-process op-store
      writes must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
      driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
      0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
      check before the mutation stage commits.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	## Todo
21	
22	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
23	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
24	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
25	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
26	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
27	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
28	 number. Long-tail entries
29	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
30	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
31	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
32	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
33	
34	1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
35	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
36	   sequence:
37	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
38	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
39	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
40	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
41	   - publish
42	
43	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
44	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
45	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
46	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
47	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
48	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
49	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
50	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
51	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
52	     normally, before the rebase.
53	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
54	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
55	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
56	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
57	     operation).
58	
59	2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
60	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
61	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
62	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
63	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
64	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
65	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
66	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
67	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
68	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
69	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
70	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
71	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
72	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
73	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
74	     not a pre-assigned version?
75	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
76	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
77	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
78	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
79	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
80	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
81	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
82	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
83	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
84	3. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
85	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
86	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
87	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
88	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
89	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
90	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
91	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
92	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
93	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
94	4. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
95	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
96	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
97	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
98	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
99	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
100	   single-mode sync cycle.
101	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
102	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
103	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
104	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
105	     nothing invokes it.
106	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
107	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
108	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
109	5. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
110	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
111	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
112	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
113	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
114	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
115	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
116	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
117	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
118	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
119	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
120	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
121	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
122	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
123	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix for.
Each entry describes what goes wrong, when, and the cost of
the failure. Entries are numbered (`1.` `2.` …) the same way
as `## Todo` in `todo.md`; run
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/bugs.md` to renumber after
insert / delete / reorder.

## Bugs

1. **`init --repo local` bare remotes keep HEAD at
   `refs/heads/master`.** The only branch pushed is `main`,
   so a later `jj git clone` of that bare repo has no default
   branch to auto-track and `vc-x1 clone` fails its
   `verify_tracking` check ("bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
   remote 'main@origin'"). Found building `tests/cli_sync.rs`
   (worked around there with `git symbolic-ref HEAD
   refs/heads/main`).
   - **Fix direction:** init's local bare provisioning sets
     HEAD to `refs/heads/main` at creation.

2. **`clone` session-remote derivation mismatches init's
   local naming; relative TARGET breaks the session clone.**
   Two related defects, both found building
   `tests/cli_sync.rs`:
   - `derive_session_url` maps `<x>.git` →
     `<x>.claude.git`, but `init --repo local` names the
     session remote `remote-claude.git` — so a dual clone of
     a locally-init'd project's `remote-code.git` looks for
     `remote-code.claude.git` and fails.
   - A relative local-path TARGET fails on the session side
     regardless: `clone_dual` runs the session `jj git
     clone` with the just-cloned code repo as cwd, so the
     relative source no longer resolves. Workaround: pass an
     absolute TARGET.

3. **`push` `bookmark-set` races the git index lock.**
   `jj bookmark set` on the colocated work repo failed twice
   (the 0.69.0-3 and 0.69.0-4 pushes, same stage) with
   "Failed to reset Git HEAD state … could not acquire lock
   for `.git/index` … after 1 attempt(s)"; the lockfile was
   already gone on inspection seconds later.
   - **Cost:** push aborts mid-flow (rollback restored both
     repos cleanly both times); recovery is a `--restart`
     rerun, which succeeded both times.
   - The bot thinks a git-aware watcher (shell prompt,
     editor) briefly re-reads the repo after the commit
     stages touch `.git`, holding the index lock exactly
     when `bookmark-set` resets git HEAD; jj gives up after
     a single attempt.
   - **Fix direction** (two options, possibly combined):
     - retry with short backoff around the bookmark-set
       stage (or all jj invocations that reset git HEAD
       state)
     - use jj-lib in-process instead of spawning `jj`
       commands (suggested 2026-07-15) — the lock
       contention is external, so this alone doesn't
       remove the race, but the retry loop becomes ours
       (catch the lock error, back off, retry) with real
       error types instead of stderr parsing; overlaps the
       jj-facade and jj-lib-vs-subprocess Todos

# References

302	    sites plus every mutation) with per-module private
303	    wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in init. Spawning means
304	    stderr parsing instead of typed errors, and jj's
305	    single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our
306	    control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race in
307	    [bugs.md](bugs.md) [[4]] can't be retried where it
308	    fails. Absorbs the former "Dedup jj subprocess queries
309	    behind a typed facade" Todo. Rough staging:

312	      template)` plus typed helpers — `rev_exists`,
313	      `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`. Existing

315	      jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
316	      `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline

322	        two tracking parsers will eventually disagree

326	        one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call

333	      git-push → re-colocate flow is the step shape the
334	      abandoned submodule design required [[2]]; the
335	      symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
336	      replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
337	      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates

17. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
    subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
    scattered hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query
    sites plus every mutation) with per-module private
    wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in init. Spawning means
    stderr parsing instead of typed errors, and jj's
    single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our
    control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race in
    [bugs.md](bugs.md) [[4]] can't be retried where it
    fails. Absorbs the former "Dedup jj subprocess queries
    behind a typed facade" Todo. Rough staging:
    - **DRY behind one facade module** (name open:
      `src/jj.rs` or a `common` split): `jj_log(repo, rev,
      template)` plus typed helpers — `rev_exists`,
      `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`. Existing
      reinventions to fold: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists,
      jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
      `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline
      `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
      template variants in `sync.rs`.
      - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
        `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
        parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
        two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
      - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
        `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
        `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
        one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
      - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
        `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
        `push/integration_tests.rs`,
        `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
        `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
    - **De-gitify init.** `init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj →
      git-push → re-colocate flow is the step shape the
      abandoned submodule design required [[2]]; the
      symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
      replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
      the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
      `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
      `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set
      main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`; no
      `--allow-new` needed, push established tracking as a
      side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
      [[3]]). Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
      `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
    - **Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib** (already a
      dependency for read-only commands): reads first
      (`jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors), then
      mutations (commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
      fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store
      lift). Removes jj and git spawning entirely; the
      index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
      retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for
      bugs.md entry 3 [[4]]. `LocalBareInit`'s
      `git init --bare` becomes a gix call (jj-lib already
      pulls gix in).
    - **Update the access-rule doc** (moved from the "Split
      push.rs state machinery" Todo): today's implicit rule
      — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
      commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
      in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
      instead.
    - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
      `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
      until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
      lands (same direction, independently shippable).
      Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
      invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
      in-process read.
    - Design risk (the bot thinks): in-process op-store
      writes must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
      driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
      0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
      check before the mutation stage commits.

## Ideas

 Items not yet solid enough for `## Todo` (or surfaced
 during close-out / end-of-day before they're fully

content.text:
The consolidation is done. Todo #18 is now **#17: "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end subprocess spawning"** (notes/todo.md:299), validated with `vc-x1 validate-todo` — 17 entries, all sequential.

What I examined and how each related todo landed:

**Folded in entirely:**
- **#9 "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a typed facade"** — deleted as a standalone entry; its full content (facade module with typed helpers, the `bm_track_one` fold, ochid-parser unification, test-helper dedup) became the first stage of the refactor. It was the natural DRY stage anyway.
- **The access-rule half of #10** — the "document jj-lib vs subprocess" bullet moved into the refactor, because the rule it would document (read-only → jj-lib, mutating → shell out) *dissolves* when mutations go in-process. Old #10 survives as #9, trimmed to just "Split push.rs state machinery" with a pointer to where the rule discussion went.
- **The old #18 de-gitify content** — now the second stage, with the verified jj-only command sequence preserved.

**Referenced but deliberately not swallowed:**
- **#4 "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"** — it removes the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn (same no-spawning direction), but it's ranked #4, has its own semantic concerns (racy preflight, rollback philosophy), and is independently shippable. Folding it would have silently demoted it to rank 17. It's cross-referenced by title instead.
- **#11 "Run validate-bot at every invocation"** — noted as a beneficiary (its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an in-process read), not a component.
- **bugs.md entry 3 (the index-lock race)** — cited as motivation; the mutation stage is its fix vehicle since the retry loop becomes ours with real error types. I also updated bugs.md's fix-direction bullet, which still named the two now-absorbed todos.

The staging in the entry is: DRY facade → de-gitify init → migrate facade internals to jj-lib (reads, then mutations) → update the ARCHITECTURE.md rule. Two things worth flagging: `gh` and `$EDITOR` spawns remain by design, and I added a marked design risk — jj-lib 0.41 in-process op-store writes coexisting with your external jj 0.40.0 CLI on the same repos needs a version-skew compatibility check before the mutation stage commits to the approach.

lastPrompt: Let's change #18 into a refactoring that includes:  - DRY the code  - Use jj-lib and removes all spawning, this inherently remove use of raw-git  - Examine other todo's and add them into this refactor…

      `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
      template variants in `sync.rs`.
      - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
        `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
        parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
        two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
      - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
        `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
        `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
        one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
      - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
        `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
        `push/integration_tests.rs`,
        `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
        `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
    - **De-gitify init.** `init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj →
      git-push → re-colocate flow is the step shape the
      abandoned submodule design required [[2]]; the
      symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
      replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
      scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
      the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
      `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
      `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set
      main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`; no
      `--allow-new` needed, push established tracking as a
      side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
      [[3]]). Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
      `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
    - **Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib** (already a
      dependency for read-only commands): reads first
      (`jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors), then
      mutations (commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
      fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store
      lift). Removes jj and git spawning entirely; the
      index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
      retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for
      bugs.md entry 3 [[4]]. `LocalBareInit`'s
      `git init --bare` becomes a gix call (jj-lib already
      pulls gix in).
    - **Update the access-rule doc** (moved from the "Split
      push.rs state machinery" Todo): today's implicit rule
      — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
      commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
      in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
      instead.
    - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
      `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
      until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
      lands (same direction, independently shippable).
      Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
      invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
      in-process read.
    - Design risk (the bot thinks): in-process op-store
      writes must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
      driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
      0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
      check before the mutation stage commits.

## Ideas

128	     normally, before the rebase.
129	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
130	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
131	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
132	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
133	     operation).
134	
135	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
136	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
137	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
138	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
139	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
140	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
141	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
142	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
143	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
144	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
145	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
146	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
147	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
148	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
149	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
150	     not a pre-assigned version?
151	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
152	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
153	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
154	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
155	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
156	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
157	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
158	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
159	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.

1	# AGENTS.md - Bot Instructions

3	## Project Structure
4	
5	This project uses **two separate jj-git repos**:
6	
7	1. **App repo** (`/` — project root): the project's generated
8	   artifact — code, prose, image, song, whatever it produces.
9	2. **Bot session repo** (`/.claude/`): Contains Claude Code session data.
10	
11	Both repos are managed with `jj` (Jujutsu), which coexists with git.
12	
13	**Committing vs pushing.** Default to `vc-x1 push` — it commits and
14	publishes both repos together, each carrying one `ochid:` pointing at
15	the other (see [Cycle Protocol](#cycle-protocol) and
16	[ochid trailers](#cross-repo-linking-ochid-trailers)). Use a bare
17	`jj commit` (see [jj Basics](#jj-basics)) only when:
18	
19	- the change will never be published — no `ochid:`;
20	- the commit will be squashed away before its series is pushed
21	  (loop-and-squash) — no `ochid:`; it disappears in the squash;
22	- the commit will be pushed later as a non-top commit — `jj commit`
23	  and add its `ochid:` now, because `vc-x1 push` stamps only the
24	  topmost commit it pushes, never the ancestors.
25	
26	**What "commit" and "push" mean.** In an instruction, "commit",
27	"push", and "commit + push" all mean `vc-x1 push` — land *and*
28	publish — unless stated otherwise. A bare `jj commit` is asked for
29	by name: "local commit", "save locally", "just `jj commit`" (the
30	cases above). So the spoken default is publish; the local-only
31	save is the named exception. The approval around a push —
32	interactive by default, waived only by an explicit scoped
33	delegation — is the cycle protocol's

62

78	- **Memory directory** — hidden under the user's home, tied to one

86	When a conversational reply centers on a technical explanation

91

93	the plain form catches misunderstandings early, when they are

103	Durable text the bot writes — AGENTS.md, `notes/`, commit bodies,

106	enters the text, prefix it with "We think ..." (a royal "we") so a

108	
109	**Why:** unmarked speculation reads like evidence, and a future reader

113

content.text:
Thoughts on both, no edits made.

## Folding 8, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17 into #1

They fit, but in two distinct ways, and the entry should say which is which:

- **Natural components** — work the refactor's own stages would churn anyway:
  - **#17 (validate-desc/fix-desc por equalization)** and **#11 (configurable bot dir name)** slot cleanly into the facade stage: once one module owns "resolve the repos and query them," topology-from-config and the `.claude`-name lookup are parameters of that facade rather than scattered preludes. These two strengthen the refactor.
  - **#15 (options_flags `value` field)** and **#14 (terminology stragglers)** are DRY/hygiene sweeps that touch the same lines big refactor commits will rewrite — cheap riders, best done early so later stages diff cleanly.
  - **#8 (push: validate body intro)** is a small validator that belongs in whatever push-stage rework the mutation stage does.
- **A genuine scope expansion**: **#16 (por → dual conversion)** is a new subcommand, not a refactoring. Folding it in turns #1 from "restructure how vc-x1 talks to jj" into "restructure vc-x1, period." That's a legitimate call — but it makes #1 a multi-ladder *program*, not one cycle, and the entry should be shaped accordingly (an ordered stage list where each stage is its own `X.Y.Z` ladder, facade first).

Two items you *didn't* list that arguably belong more than #16: **#10 (split push.rs)** — the mutation stage will gut push.rs anyway, and doing the split first makes that reviewable — and **#12 (validate-bot every invocation)**, which I'd leave out but which is a one-bullet beneficiary already noted. Worth a yes/no from you on #10 when I make the edit.

## The trapezoid / no-ff close-out merge

This is a good fit for the refactor, and it connects two existing threads: Todo #2's "pause point" exists precisely because today's merge close-out is a manual recipe, and AGENTS.md already documents the ochid shape for it (bot-side commit carries one `ochid:` per work commit in the cycle). Automating it retires the workaround at the source. Specifics worth deciding up front:

- **Parent order matters.** You described parents as (last `-X` commit, parent-of-first-ladder-commit). I'd recommend the reverse: **first parent = the pre-cycle base, second parent = the ladder tip** — the git `merge --no-ff` convention. Then `git log --first-parent main` reads as one commit per cycle (the squash-like view), while the full ladder stays reachable through the second parent. Tip-first would put the whole ladder in the first-parent walk and lose that payoff.
- **Mechanics are easy; stamping is the work.** The merge commit itself is trivial in jj (`jj new <base> <tip>` + describe) and even easier in-process with jj-lib (a two-parent commit in one transaction). The real feature is the ochid bookkeeping: the code-side merge carries its bot ochid, the bot commit carries N code ochids — and push today stamps only the topmost commit. That's exactly the machinery the jj-lib mutation stage rebuilds, which is why this belongs in the refactor rather than as a bolt-on to current push.
- **Validators need to learn the shape.** `validate-desc` and the push sanity verifiers assume linear 1:1 commits; a two-parent close-out with N ochids is a new legal shape they must accept — one more reason #17's validator work being folded in is convenient.
- **Empty-tree worry is a non-issue**: since the base is an ancestor of the tip, the merge's tree is just the tip's tree — no conflicts possible.
- **Todo #2 interaction**: the pause-point + manual-rebase plan in #2 is the interim recipe; this feature is its end state. #2 should either fold in as the same stage or stay as the near-term stopgap explicitly marked as retired-by-#1.

**The plain version:** each cycle's commits would stay on a side path, and finishing the cycle adds one tie-off commit that joins the side path back to the main line. Looking at just the main line you'd see one entry per cycle; the detailed steps are still there if you follow the side path. The tricky part isn't creating that commit — it's making sure all the cross-repo link stamps land on it correctly, which is exactly the plumbing this refactor rebuilds.

Say the word and I'll fold the six in (plus #10 if you want it) and add the trapezoid close-out as a stage of the mutation work.

content.text: Folding them in now. First let me read the full text of the entries I haven't seen completely (#8, #11, #14, #16).

input.description: Read entries 8, 11-12, 14, 16 in full

content.content:
8. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
   0.62.0-5.
   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
     to accept bullet-first bodies.
   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
11. **Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
    hardcoded `.claude`).** The bot repo lives at `.claude`
    because that is where Claude Code keeps session data,
    but the concept is agent-agnostic and the user wants to
    change it (decided 2026-07-15: the name does not need to
    be `.claude`). Today the literal is embedded across
    init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
    semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
    `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs.
    - `.vc-config.toml` already records each repo's
      workspace path — likely the natural home for the
      configurable name.
    - Open: migration for existing workspaces, the ochid
      trailer prefix (recorded in immutable history), and
      the Claude Code symlink whose location the harness
      controls.
12. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
    (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
    (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
    to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
    "could, not should". Design points:
    - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
      shares the lookup with the configurable-name Todo
      above) and silently skip when absent
    - severity knob in `.vc-config.toml`
      (`warn|error|off`): unrelated commands (fix-todo)
      warn at most; push / squash-push / validate-bot
      already have their own handling from 0.69.0-3
13. **README: audit flag tables and examples against the
    current CLI.** 0.69.0-4 fixed the init section (it
    documented retired `--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local`
    flags) and the 0.69.0 surfaces, but the README's other
    tables (clone, symlink, sync, revert, list/desc/chid/
    show) have never had a systematic `-h` comparison and
    drift silently.
    - Sweep each section against `vc-x1 <cmd> -h`.
    - Consider regenerating transcripts via support
      scripts (the gen-exmpl pattern) so examples stay
      reproducible.
14. **Terminology stragglers from the work/bot sweep.** The
    0.69.0-4 sweep renamed prose and push stage names but
    left identifier-level uses of the old vocabulary:
    - `-s` scope keyword `code` (CLI value — renaming to
      `work` is a breaking change; `bot` already matches)
    - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
      `remote-claude.git`
    - identifiers: `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
      `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
    - decide rename vs document-as-historical; overlaps
      "Make the bot repo directory name configurable"
      above
16. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
    companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
    workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
    setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
    proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
    Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
17. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization.**
    Replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
    subcommands (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`)
    with a scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot`
    when absent. Body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit/design
    work [[13]] identified this as the cheapest concrete
    equalization and the right prototype for the
    topology-from-config rule (subcommand reads topology
    from `default_scope`, not from a flag). Validates the
    broader design before larger pieces (`push`,
    `--init-from*`) commit to it. The remaining 13
    implementation gaps live in [[13]]'s `## Gap list` for
    future Preparation passes to pick up.
## Ideas
