input.old_string:
1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
   scattered hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query
   sites plus every mutation) with per-module private
   wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in init. Spawning means
   stderr parsing instead of typed errors, and jj's
   single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our
   control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race in
   [bugs.md](bugs.md) [[4]] can't be retried where it
   fails. Absorbs the former "Dedup jj subprocess queries
   behind a typed facade" Todo. Rough staging:
   - **DRY behind one facade module** (name open:
     `src/jj.rs` or a `common` split): `jj_log(repo, rev,
     template)` plus typed helpers — `rev_exists`,
     `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`. Existing
     reinventions to fold: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists,
     jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
     `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline
     `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
     template variants in `sync.rs`.
     - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
       `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
       parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
       two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
     - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
       `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
       `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
       one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
     - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
       `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
       `push/integration_tests.rs`,
       `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
       `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
   - **De-gitify init.** `init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj →
     git-push → re-colocate flow is the step shape the
     abandoned submodule design required [[2]]; the
     symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
     replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
     scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
     the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
     `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
     `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set
     main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`; no
     `--allow-new` needed, push established tracking as a
     side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
     [[3]]). Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
     `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
   - **Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib** (already a
     dependency for read-only commands): reads first
     (`jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors), then
     mutations (commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
     fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store
     lift). Removes jj and git spawning entirely; the
     index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
     retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for
     bugs.md entry 3 [[4]]. `LocalBareInit`'s
     `git init --bare` becomes a gix call (jj-lib already
     pulls gix in).
   - **Update the access-rule doc** (moved from the "Split
     push.rs state machinery" Todo): today's implicit rule
     — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
     commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
     in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
     instead.
   - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
     `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
     until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
     lands (same direction, independently shippable).
     Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
     invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
     in-process read.
   - Design risk (the bot thinks): in-process op-store
     writes must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
     driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
     0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
     check before the mutation stage commits.
input.new_string:
1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
   scattered hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query
   sites plus every mutation) with per-module private
   wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in init. Spawning means
   stderr parsing instead of typed errors, and jj's
   single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our
   control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race in
   [bugs.md](bugs.md) [[4]] can't be retried where it
   fails. A multi-ladder program: each stage below is
   roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle, in order, facade first.
   Absorbs the former Todos "Dedup jj subprocess queries
   behind a typed facade", "Split push.rs state
   machinery", "vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an
   intro paragraph", "Make the bot repo directory name
   configurable", "Terminology stragglers from the
   work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags` leaves →
   `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion", and
   "`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".
   - **Stage: DRY behind one facade module** (name open:
     `src/jj.rs` or a `common` split): `jj_log(repo, rev,
     template)` plus typed helpers — `rev_exists`,
     `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`. Existing
     reinventions to fold: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists,
     jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
     `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline
     `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
     template variants in `sync.rs`.
     - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
       `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
       parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
       two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
     - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
       `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
       `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
       one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
     - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
       `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
       `push/integration_tests.rs`,
       `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
       `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
   - **Stage: hygiene riders** — sweeps that churn the same
     lines the facade stage rewrites, done early so later
     stages diff cleanly:
     - terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot
       sweep: `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to
       `work` is a breaking CLI change; `bot` already
       matches), init's literal remote names
       `remote-code.git` / `remote-claude.git`,
       identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
       `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`; decide rename vs
       document-as-historical (overlaps the configurable
       bot-dir-name stage below)
     - single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field
       (the 0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf):
       sweep `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
       `config`, `use_template` + consumers (`init.rs`,
       tests). Open: could a single field be a type/enum
       instead of a struct, eliminating the
       `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely
   - **Stage: facade owns topology** — repo resolution
     becomes facade state, not per-command preludes:
     - `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization:
       replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude in
       both (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`)
       with scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot`
       when absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit
       [[13]] called this the cheapest equalization and
       the prototype for the topology-from-config rule
       (topology from `default_scope`, not a flag); the
       remaining implementation gaps live in [[13]]'s
       `## Gap list`
     - make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
       hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name
       need not be `.claude`). The literal is embedded
       across init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid
       path semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
       `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
       `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths —
       the natural home. Open: migration of existing
       workspaces, the ochid prefix recorded in immutable
       history, the harness-controlled symlink location
   - **Stage: de-gitify init.** `init.rs::push_repo`'s
     strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate flow is the step
     shape the abandoned submodule design required [[2]];
     the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted. jj-only
     replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
     scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates
     the git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
     `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
     `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set
     main -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`; no
     `--allow-new` needed, push established tracking as a
     side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
     [[3]]). Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
     `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
   - **Stage: split push.rs.** `push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the
     largest file) holds the `Stage` machine, TOML state
     persistence, eight stage bodies, two sanity
     verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
     is the in-repo precedent. Extract `push/state.rs`
     (`Stage`, `PushState`, state layout resolution) so
     the mutation stage below rewrites stage bodies in a
     file that reviews cleanly.
   - **Stage: migrate facade internals to jj-lib** (already
     a dependency for read-only commands): reads first
     (`jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors), then
     mutations (commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
     fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store
     lift). Removes jj and git spawning entirely; the
     index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
     retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for
     bugs.md entry 3 [[4]]. `LocalBareInit`'s
     `git init --bare` becomes a gix call (jj-lib already
     pulls gix in).
   - **Stage: push features on the rebuilt plumbing:**
     - validate the body opens with an intro paragraph
       (Prose Form): a bullet-first body trips jj's arg
       parser today (`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-`
       as a stray flag; hit on 0.62.0-5) and push fails
       with an opaque error. Validate and report the
       offending first line with a clear error instead —
       enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, not a
       parser fix. Workaround until then: prepend a
       non-dash intro sentence.
     - trapezoid (merge non-ff) close-out: the close-out
       commit becomes a two-parent merge — first parent
       the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's
       first commit), second parent the ladder tip
       (`-X`), the `git merge --no-ff` convention — so
       `git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per
       cycle while the full ladder stays reachable via
       the second parent. The merge itself is trivial
       in-process (a two-parent commit in one
       transaction; no conflicts possible since the base
       is an ancestor of the tip). The real work is ochid
       stamping — the bot commit carries one ochid per
       work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md merge
       non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
       topmost commit — and teaching `validate-desc` +
       the push sanity verifiers the new legal shape.
       End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point
       between commit and publish stages", which stays as
       the interim manual recipe.
   - **Stage: `por → dual` conversion.** Attach a bot
     companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing
     por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
     Manual setup on an external por workspace
     (2026-05-14) proved arduous; should be a routine
     subcommand. Design stub in [[1]] § 2. Last on
     purpose: it leans on facade topology and the
     in-process init pieces.
   - **Update the access-rule doc**: today's implicit rule
     — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
     commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
     in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
     instead.
   - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
     `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
     until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
     lands (same direction, independently shippable).
     Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
     invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
     in-process read.
   - Design risk (we think): in-process op-store writes
     must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
     driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
     0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
     check before the mutation stage commits.

input.old_string:
8. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
   0.62.0-5.
   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
     to accept bullet-first bodies.
   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
     non-dash intro sentence to the body.

input.old_string:
10. **Split push.rs state machinery.** `push.rs` (~1.6k
    lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage` machine, TOML
    state persistence, eight stage bodies, two sanity
    verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs` is
    the in-repo precedent for splitting.
    - extract `push/state.rs`: `Stage`, `PushState`, state
      layout resolution
    - the jj-lib-vs-subprocess access-rule documentation
      that used to live here moved into the "Refactor:
      typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process" Todo below
11. **Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
    hardcoded `.claude`).** The bot repo lives at `.claude`
    because that is where Claude Code keeps session data,
    but the concept is agent-agnostic and the user wants to
    change it (decided 2026-07-15: the name does not need to
    be `.claude`). Today the literal is embedded across
    init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
    semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
    `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs.
    - `.vc-config.toml` already records each repo's
      workspace path — likely the natural home for the
      configurable name.
    - Open: migration for existing workspaces, the ochid
      trailer prefix (recorded in immutable history), and
      the Claude Code symlink whose location the harness
      controls.
12. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
    (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
    (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
    to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
    "could, not should". Design points:
    - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
      shares the lookup with the configurable-name Todo
      above) and silently skip when absent

input.old_string:
14. **Terminology stragglers from the work/bot sweep.** The
    0.69.0-4 sweep renamed prose and push stage names but
    left identifier-level uses of the old vocabulary:
    - `-s` scope keyword `code` (CLI value — renaming to
      `work` is a breaking change; `bot` already matches)
    - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
      `remote-claude.git`
    - identifiers: `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
      `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
    - decide rename vs document-as-historical; overlaps
      "Make the bot repo directory name configurable"
      above
15. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
    `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
    its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
    so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
    on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
    leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
    `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
    (`init.rs`, tests).

    Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
    of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
    issue.
16. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
    companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
    workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
    setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
    proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
    Design stub in [[1]] § 2.
17. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization.**
    Replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
    subcommands (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`)
    with a scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot`
    when absent. Body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit/design
    work [[13]] identified this as the cheapest concrete
    equalization and the right prototype for the
    topology-from-config rule (subcommand reads topology
    from `default_scope`, not from a flag). Validates the
    broader design before larger pieces (`push`,
    `--init-from*`) commit to it. The remaining 13
    implementation gaps live in [[13]]'s `## Gap list` for
    future Preparation passes to pick up.

## Ideas
input.new_string: ## Ideas

content.text:
Done. All seven entries are folded into #1 and the trapezoid close-out is in. The final state:

**Todo #1** is now a multi-ladder program with an explicit stage order — each stage roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle:

1. **DRY facade** (former "Dedup jj subprocess queries")
2. **Hygiene riders** — terminology stragglers + `options_flags` `value`-field sweep, done early so later stages diff cleanly
3. **Facade owns topology** — validate-desc/fix-desc por equalization (the topology-from-config prototype) + configurable bot-dir name
4. **De-gitify init** (the verified jj-only flow)
5. **Split push.rs** — extracted before the mutation stage guts the stage bodies, so that rewrite reviews cleanly
6. **jj-lib migration** — reads then mutations; the lock-race fix vehicle
7. **Push features on the rebuilt plumbing** — body-intro validation + the **trapezoid close-out**: two-parent merge with first parent = pre-cycle base, second = ladder tip (the `git merge --no-ff` convention, so `git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle), with the real work called out as ochid stamping (one per work commit on the bot side) and teaching the validators the new legal shape
8. **por → dual conversion** — deliberately last, since it leans on facade topology and in-process init
9. Access-rule doc update, still-spawned list, and the jj-lib/jj-CLI version-skew design risk

**Cross-references reconciled:** the pause-point entry (now #2) gained a bullet marking it as the interim manual recipe with the trapezoid stage as its end state; validate-bot (now #9) had its "configurable-name Todo above" pointer redirected into the refactor's stage; and the absorb list in #1 names all eight former titles for greppability. The list renumbered to 10 entries (#2 push pause point, #3 version-number protocol, … #10 README audit) and `validate-todo` passes — 10 entries, all sequential.

One small thing I carried along: the design-risk marker now reads "we think" to match the updated Speculation-marker wording in AGENTS.md.

message.content: LG, but let's create refator-20260716.md as that change is too big and unreadble. I recommend in refactor-xx.md each sub task be a .md section (## title) that way it can be referenced in chores and else where in the future. Thoughts no changes.

content.text:
Fully agree — the entry as it stands breaks the project's own rule (todo.md's intro: entries are "a short description and reference links to more details"), and it's the same disease the 0.58.0 split cured. Thoughts on the shape:

**What the file should be.** `notes/refactor-20260716.md` becomes the program's plan-and-design document: a short intro (the problem statement — spawning, stderr parsing, the lock race), then one `##` section per stage. Each section gets a stable GitHub anchor, so chores sections, todo entries, and bugs.md can point at `refactor-20260716.md#stage-…` with the existing `[N]:` reference machinery — exactly what a numbered todo entry can never offer. It also gives design content room to grow (the trapezoid parent-order rationale, the jj-lib version-skew check results) without inflating anything.

**What stays in todo.md #1.** A few lines: title, one-sentence problem, "staged plan in [refactor-20260716.md]", maybe a one-line-per-stage checklist. The `[[2]]/[[3]]/[[13]]`-style refs migrate into the new file's own file-local `# References` (renumbered locally, per the footnote convention); todo.md's freed slots get pruned if nothing else cites them.

**Division of labor with chores — worth deciding up front.** Chores stays the record of *landed* work (commits, narrative per cycle); the refactor file is the *forward* plan. So: when a stage ships, its chores-NN.md section records it as usual, and the refactor file's stage section gets a one-line status + link to that chores section — not a duplicate of the narrative. Same "git owns the mechanics, chores owns the story" spirit, extended one level: refactor file owns the program design.

**Two small suggestions:**
- Section titles should be short and rename-resistant (`## Stage: DRY facade`, `## Stage: trapezoid close-out`), since every future ref hardcodes the anchor. These are design sections, not commit-recording headers, so free-form names are correct under the existing convention.
- On the name: `refactor-20260716.md` works; the one cost of date-only naming is that a future reader can't tell what's inside from `ls`. If you expect more refactor programs later, `refactor-20260716-jj-lib.md` keeps the dated series *and* the content hint. Your call — I'll use whichever you pick.

Also worth a line in `notes/README.md` (it indexes the notes files) when I create it. Say go and I'll build the file, shrink #1, and rewire the refs.

223	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
224	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
225	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
226	     operation).
227	   - Interim recipe only: the trapezoid close-out stage of
228	     the refactor Todo above is the end state; this pause
229	     point remains the manual path until it lands.
230	
231	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
232	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
233	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`

file.content:
- **Stage: push features on the rebuilt plumbing:**
     - validate the body opens with an intro paragraph
       (Prose Form): a bullet-first body trips jj's arg
       parser today (`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-`
       as a stray flag; hit on 0.62.0-5) and push fails
       with an opaque error. Validate and report the
       offending first line with a clear error instead —
       enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, not a
       parser fix. Workaround until then: prepend a
       non-dash intro sentence.
     - trapezoid (merge non-ff) close-out: the close-out
       commit becomes a two-parent merge — first parent
       the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's
       first commit), second parent the ladder tip
       (`-X`), the `git merge --no-ff` convention — so
       `git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per
       cycle while the full ladder stays reachable via
       the second parent. The merge itself is trivial
       in-process (a two-parent commit in one
       transaction; no conflicts possible since the base
       is an ancestor of the tip). The real work is ochid
       stamping — the bot commit carries one ochid per
       work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md merge
       non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
       topmost commit — and teaching `validate-desc` +
       the push sanity verifiers the new legal shape.
       End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point
       between commit and publish stages", which stays as
       the interim manual recipe.
   - **Stage: `por → dual` conversion.** Attach a bot
     companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing
     por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
     Manual setup on an external por workspace
     (2026-05-14) proved arduous; should be a routine
     subcommand. Design stub in [[1]] § 2. Last on
     purpose: it leans on facade topology and the
     in-process init pieces.
   - **Update the access-rule doc**: today's implicit rule
     — read-only display commands use jj-lib, mutating
     commands shell out — dissolves once mutations are
     in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the end state
     instead.
   - Still spawned afterward: `gh` (remote provisioning),
     `$EDITOR`, and the `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn
     until "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process"
     lands (same direction, independently shippable).
     Beneficiary: "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
     invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
     in-process read.
   - Design risk (we think): in-process op-store writes
     must coexist with the user's external `jj` CLI
     driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41 vs installed jj
     0.40.0 format/version skew needs a compatibility
     check before the mutation stage commits.
2. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).
   - Interim recipe only: the trapezoid close-out stage of
     the refactor Todo above is the end state; this pause
     point remains the manual path until it lands.

3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`

# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are todo.md and the chores-NN.md files in
[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks and
todo.md contains short term tasks and their status. The
chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; the highest-numbered
file is the active one, older ones are closed.

Tool architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in the chores files) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow and conventions live in
[`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md):

- [Notes file conventions](../AGENTS.md#notes-file-conventions)
  — Todo format, Reference numbering, Notes references
  (`[[N]]` citation style), Markdown anchor links, Retiring
  Done entries.
- [Chores conventions](../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions) —
  section headers / Done entries exact-title rule, content
  rules, `Commits:` line format.
- [Code Conventions](../AGENTS.md#code-conventions) — doc
  comments, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.

Per-cycle workflow lives in
[`cycle-protocol.md`](cycle-protocol.md):

- [Cycles](cycle-protocol.md#cycles) — three-phase shape
  (Preparation → Work → Close-out), `X.Y.Z-N` numbering,
  sub-cycles.
- [Per-commit flow](cycle-protocol.md#per-commit-flow) —
  cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`),
  work + commit description review gates.
- [Commit description](cycle-protocol.md#commit-description)
  — Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape
  per work vs bot repo.
- [Pushing](cycle-protocol.md#pushing) — push policy,
  close-out shape, `.claude` cadence.

input.content:
# Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process (2026-07-16)

The plan-and-design document for the jj refactor program.
Version-control operations are scattered hand-rolled
`run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query sites plus every mutation)
with per-module private wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in
init. Spawning means stderr parsing instead of typed errors,
and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside
our control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]] can't
be retried where it fails.

How this file works:

- Each stage below is a `##` section in execution order;
  each is roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle. Section anchors are the
  durable way to reference a stage from todo.md, chores, or
  bugs.md.
- This file owns the forward plan and design. When a stage
  ships, its `chores-NN.md` section records the commits and
  narrative as usual; the stage section here gets a one-line
  status link to that chores section — no duplication.
- The `## Todo` entry "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib
  in-process; end subprocess spawning" in
  [todo.md](todo.md) is the short pointer here.

Absorbed former Todos: "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a
typed facade", "Split push.rs state machinery", "vc-x1 push:
validate body opens with an intro paragraph", "Make the bot
repo directory name configurable", "Terminology stragglers
from the work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags`
leaves → `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion",
"`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".

## Stage: DRY facade

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper. Spotted at
0.69.0-2: `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is
the Nth reinvention.

- One facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a `common`
  split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus typed helpers —
  `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`.
- Existing reinventions to fold:
  `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
  rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` + four
  inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
  three template variants in `sync.rs`.
- Fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command` +
  its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
  `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking parsers
  will eventually disagree.
- Unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
  `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
  (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
  `desc_helpers` both can call.
- Test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()` /
  `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
  `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
  and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`.

## Stage: hygiene riders

Sweeps that churn the same lines the facade stage rewrites;
done early so later stages diff cleanly.

- Terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot sweep:
  - `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to `work` is a
    breaking CLI change; `bot` already matches)
  - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
    `remote-claude.git`
  - identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
    `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
  - decide rename vs document-as-historical (overlaps
    [Stage: facade owns topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology))
- Single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field (the
  0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf): sweep `repo`,
  `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`
  + consumers (`init.rs`, tests). Open: could a single field
  be a type/enum instead of a struct, eliminating the
  `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely.

## Stage: facade owns topology

Repo resolution becomes facade state, not per-command
preludes.

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization: replace the
  `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`) with
  scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when
  absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit [[2]] called this
  the cheapest equalization and the prototype for the
  topology-from-config rule (topology from `default_scope`,
  not a flag); the remaining implementation gaps live in
  [[2]]'s `## Gap list`.
- Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
  hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name need not
  be `.claude`). The literal is embedded across
  init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
  semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
  `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
  `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Open: migration of existing workspaces, the
  ochid prefix recorded in immutable history, the
  harness-controlled symlink location.

## Stage: de-gitify init

`init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate
flow is the step shape the abandoned submodule design
required [[3]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.

- jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
  scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates the
  git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
  `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
  `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
  -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- No `--allow-new` needed; push established tracking as a
  side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
  [[4]]).
- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.

## Stage: split push.rs

`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
is the in-repo precedent.

- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
  layout resolution) so the
  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.

## Stage: jj-lib migration

Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
entirely.

- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).

## Stage: push body-intro validation

A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.

- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
  behavior, not a parser fix.
- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.

## Stage: trapezoid close-out

The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.

- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
  base is an ancestor of the tip.
- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
  topmost commit.
- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
- End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
  commit and publish stages", which stays as the interim
  manual recipe until this lands.

## Stage: por → dual conversion

Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
in [[5]] § 2.

- Last on purpose: it leans on
  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
  in-process init pieces.

## Access rule

Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
end state instead.

## Out of scope: still spawned

- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
  design.
- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
  program.
- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
  in-process read.

## Design risk: op-store coexistence

We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
compatibility check before the
[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
approach.

# References

[1]: /notes/bugs.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.

## Stage: split push.rs

`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
is the in-repo precedent.

- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
  layout resolution) so the
  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.

## Stage: jj-lib migration

Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
entirely.

- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).

## Stage: push body-intro validation

A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.

- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
  behavior, not a parser fix.
- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.

## Stage: trapezoid close-out

The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.

- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
  base is an ancestor of the tip.
- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
  topmost commit.
- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
- End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
  commit and publish stages", which stays as the interim
  manual recipe until this lands.

## Stage: por → dual conversion

Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
in [[5]] § 2.

- Last on purpose: it leans on
  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
  in-process init pieces.

## Access rule

Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
end state instead.

## Out of scope: still spawned

- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
  design.
- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
  program.
- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
  in-process read.

## Design risk: op-store coexistence

We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
compatibility check before the
[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
approach.

# References

[1]: /notes/bugs.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

   single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside our

     reinventions to fold: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists,
     jj_commit_id, rev_is_empty_undescribed}`,
     `push::jj_log_empty` + four inline
     `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`, three
     template variants in `sync.rs`.
     - fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw
       `std::process::Command` + its own `@origin:` prefix
       parse) onto `common::verify_tracking`'s parser —
       two tracking parsers will eventually disagree
     - unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
       `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs
       `common::extract_ochid` (jj-lib `Commit`-based);
       one string-level parser in `desc_helpers` both call
     - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` /
       `cid()` / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
       `push/integration_tests.rs`,
       `sync/integration_tests.rs`, and
       `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`
   - **Stage: hygiene riders** — sweeps that churn the same
     lines the facade stage rewrites, done early so later
     stages diff cleanly:
     - terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot
       sweep: `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to
       `work` is a breaking CLI change; `bot` already
       matches), init's literal remote names
       `remote-code.git` / `remote-claude.git`,
       identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
       `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`; decide rename vs
       document-as-historical (overlaps the configurable
       bot-dir-name stage below)
     - single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field
       (the 0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf):
       sweep `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
       `config`, `use_template` + consumers (`init.rs`,
       tests). Open: could a single field be a type/enum
       instead of a struct, eliminating the
       `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely
   - **Stage: facade owns topology** — repo resolution
     becomes facade state, not per-command preludes:
     - `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization:
       replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude in
       both (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`)
       with scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot`
       when absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit
       [[13]] called this the cheapest equalization and
       the prototype for the topology-from-config rule
       (topology from `default_scope`, not a flag); the
       remaining implementation gaps live in [[13]]'s
       `## Gap list`
     - make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
       hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix for.
Each entry describes what goes wrong, when, and the cost of
the failure. Entries are numbered (`1.` `2.` …) the same way
as `## Todo` in `todo.md`; run
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/bugs.md` to renumber after
insert / delete / reorder.

## Bugs

1. **`init --repo local` bare remotes keep HEAD at
   `refs/heads/master`.** The only branch pushed is `main`,
   so a later `jj git clone` of that bare repo has no default
   branch to auto-track and `vc-x1 clone` fails its
   `verify_tracking` check ("bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
   remote 'main@origin'"). Found building `tests/cli_sync.rs`
   (worked around there with `git symbolic-ref HEAD
   refs/heads/main`).
   - **Fix direction:** init's local bare provisioning sets
     HEAD to `refs/heads/main` at creation.

2. **`clone` session-remote derivation mismatches init's
   local naming; relative TARGET breaks the session clone.**
   Two related defects, both found building
   `tests/cli_sync.rs`:
   - `derive_session_url` maps `<x>.git` →
     `<x>.claude.git`, but `init --repo local` names the
     session remote `remote-claude.git` — so a dual clone of
     a locally-init'd project's `remote-code.git` looks for
     `remote-code.claude.git` and fails.
   - A relative local-path TARGET fails on the session side
     regardless: `clone_dual` runs the session `jj git
     clone` with the just-cloned code repo as cwd, so the
     relative source no longer resolves. Workaround: pass an
     absolute TARGET.

3. **`push` `bookmark-set` races the git index lock.**
   `jj bookmark set` on the colocated work repo failed twice
   (the 0.69.0-3 and 0.69.0-4 pushes, same stage) with
   "Failed to reset Git HEAD state … could not acquire lock
   for `.git/index` … after 1 attempt(s)"; the lockfile was
   already gone on inspection seconds later.
   - **Cost:** push aborts mid-flow (rollback restored both
     repos cleanly both times); recovery is a `--restart`
     rerun, which succeeded both times.
   - The bot thinks a git-aware watcher (shell prompt,
     editor) briefly re-reads the repo after the commit
     stages touch `.git`, holding the index lock exactly
     when `bookmark-set` resets git HEAD; jj gives up after
     a single attempt.
   - **Fix direction** (two options, possibly combined):
     - retry with short backoff around the bookmark-set
       stage (or all jj invocations that reset git HEAD
       state)
     - use jj-lib in-process instead of spawning `jj`
       commands (suggested 2026-07-15) — the lock
       contention is external, so this alone doesn't
       remove the race, but the retry loop becomes ours
       (catch the lock error, back off, retry) with real
       error types instead of stderr parsing; this is the
       mutation stage of the Todo "Refactor: typed jj
       facade → jj-lib in-process; end subprocess
       spawning" (which absorbed the former jj-facade and
       jj-lib-vs-subprocess Todos)

# References

# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are todo.md and the chores-NN.md files in
[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks and
todo.md contains short term tasks and their status. The
chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; the highest-numbered
file is the active one, older ones are closed.

Tool architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in the chores files) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow and conventions live in
[`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md):

- [Notes file conventions](../AGENTS.md#notes-file-conventions)
  — Todo format, Reference numbering, Notes references
  (`[[N]]` citation style), Markdown anchor links, Retiring
  Done entries.
- [Chores conventions](../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions) —
  section headers / Done entries exact-title rule, content
  rules, `Commits:` line format.
- [Code Conventions](../AGENTS.md#code-conventions) — doc
  comments, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.

Per-cycle workflow lives in
[`cycle-protocol.md`](cycle-protocol.md):

- [Cycles](cycle-protocol.md#cycles) — three-phase shape
  (Preparation → Work → Close-out), `X.Y.Z-N` numbering,
  sub-cycles.
- [Per-commit flow](cycle-protocol.md#per-commit-flow) —
  cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`),
  work + commit description review gates.
- [Commit description](cycle-protocol.md#commit-description)
  — Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape
  per work vs bot repo.
- [Pushing](cycle-protocol.md#pushing) — push policy,
  close-out shape, `.claude` cadence.

content.text:
All done. Summary of the restructure:

**New file: `notes/refactor-20260716.md`** — the program's plan-and-design document. Short intro (problem statement plus how the file works: stages in execution order, one `##` section each, chores records shipped cycles and the stage section gets a status link back — no duplication), the absorbed-Todos list for greppability, then nine stage sections plus three coda sections (`## Access rule`, `## Out of scope: still spawned`, `## Design risk: op-store coexistence`). The push-features stage split into two sections — `## Stage: push body-intro validation` and `## Stage: trapezoid close-out` — since each is an independently referencable sub-task. It has its own file-local `# References` (`[1]`–`[5]`, renumbered in first-citation order per the footnote convention).

**Todo #1 shrank** from ~170 lines to 16: title, three-sentence problem statement, the link to the plan file, and a one-bullet stage sequence for at-a-glance orientation.

**References rewired:**
- Todo #2 (pause point) and #9 (validate-bot) now link directly to `refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out` and `#stage-facade-owns-topology` — the durable anchors a numbered todo entry could never provide.
- bugs.md entry 3's fix direction points at `#stage-jj-lib-migration`.
- todo.md's now-uncited ref definitions (`[1]`–`[4]`, `[13]`) are pruned, freeing those slots; the surviving defs (`[10]`, `[20]`, `[23]`, `[25]`) are all still cited.
- `notes/README.md` gained a paragraph establishing the pattern: multi-cycle programs get a dated plan file with per-stage anchors.

`validate-todo` passes — 10 entries, all sequential. One optional loose end: todo.md's reference namespace is now non-contiguous (`[10]`, `[20]`, `[23]`, `[25]`), which the conventions say can be re-packed opportunistically — I left it since the drift isn't bad yet and re-packing touches the Done section too.

message.content:
Note, while we're working together here I'm also working with you on ../vc-template-x1
and over there and one of the things we're doing over there is standardizing on
"work repo" and "bot repo". The reason for selecting work over app is that "app" or
"code" is associated with programming. But git repos are useful for other types of
"work" so I decided on the term "work repo". You're welcome to push back or suggest
another term. But for now that's where we are.

Thoughts, no changes ATM.

content.text:
Good context, and I'm on board with "work repo" — thoughts, no edits:

**The case for "work" holds up.** The axis that actually separates the two repos is *the deliverable* versus *the record of producing it*, and "work" captures the deliverable side without smuggling in a domain: a manuscript, a dataset, a song, and a codebase are all work; only some of them are "apps" or "code". The strongest alternative on that same axis is "product repo" (product vs process), but "work repo" is shorter, reads more naturally in prose ("the work repo's bookmark"), and pairs cleanly with "bot repo". One minor collision worth knowing about and shrugging off: git/jj vocabulary already uses "working copy"/"worktree", so "work repo" sits near an overloaded word — but in practice the contexts don't overlap enough to confuse.

**It also matches what this repo already did.** The 0.69.0-4 commit here was literally the "work/bot terminology sweep" — vc-x1's prose and push stage names already say work/bot, and the refactor's hygiene-riders stage queues the identifier-level stragglers (`Side::Code`, `claude_path`, `remote-code.git`, the `-s code` scope keyword). So the vc-template-x1 decision converges with this repo's existing direction rather than adding a new one.

**Two consistency wrinkles to note for later** (no changes now, as asked):
- Today's AGENTS.md edit here moved Project Structure to "App repo" / "Bot session repo" — which now diverges from both the template decision and this repo's own 0.69.0-4 sweep. When you're settled, that's a cheap prose fix to bring back to "work repo"; and if "bot repo" is the standard, the one-off "Bot session repo" phrasing should collapse to match.
- The hygiene stage flags `-s` scope keyword `code` → `work` as a breaking CLI change. A cross-project standard strengthens the case for actually taking that break (or accepting both keywords for a deprecation window) rather than documenting `code` as historical.

One genuine push-back candidate I considered and rejected: "primary/secondary repo" — topology-accurate but sterile, and it loses the *why* that work/bot carries. I'd stay where you landed.
